dialogos.texto_en: (The quintuplets' father is in Okinawa.)
(After receiving the news from Ichika,\n we immediately gathered and held an emergency meeting.)
(According to the information that Ichika received,\n it seems that he came to Okinawa\n to attend a conference.)
(Although the email that was sent to us stated\n that he has no plans to meet with us...)
(With a hint of anxiety, we decided\n to follow Yotsuba's plan and go sightseeing\n at Shurijo Castle tomorrow.)
(Their dad's attending the conference,\n so it's unlikely that we'll run into him.)
Well, what do you think, Uesugi-san?
I want to believe that it's someone else,\nbut it seems unlikely.
Could it be that it's just a hallucination that only\nyou can see? Like when you think of something so much\nthat anyone starts looking like our dad.
I'll check, just in case.
Hey, Yotsuba! Your ribbon! He might recognize us!
HUH?!
Let's head back.
Roger that!
He doesn't seem to be moving\nfrom the front of the Seiden.
Is he waiting for someone?
Maybe it's us?\nHahaha.
Oh come on, that's impossible.\nAhahaha.
*sigh*\nWhat are we supposed to do...?
Ah, he's back!\nHey, you two!
SHHHH!\nDon't speak so loudly!
Don't worry, Uesugi-san.\nI don't think he heard us.
I can tell from the reaction.\nDad was there.
The bad feelings about things like this\nusually turn out to be true, huh.
Sending out scouts was the right choice.
Yet, he said he wouldn't meet up with us, right?
Actually, he didn't come to meet up with us.\nEven if we do run into him, it'll be a coincidence.
Is waiting for him also a coincidence?
Raiha-chan, please stay the way you are,\nhonest and straightforward.
Huh? Huhhh?
So, what about his academic conference?\nDo they really have the luxury to be here?
Oh, about that.
Well, I was suspicious of how convenient this trip was,\nso I asked Ebata-san, and%p-2;――
Apparently, there's no academic conference in Okinawa\non Dad's schedule.
That means he definitely came here\nto keep an eye on our graduation trip.
B-but if he didn't know our itinerary,\nhow could he have known we'd be here?
Ah.
...Yotsuba?
I gave Dad a copy of our trip itinerary.
Why...?
Even though we had permission,\nI thought that Dad would still be worried.
I just wanted to let him know\nthat we won't do anything dangerous\nand he can rest easy... Ahaha...
I'm just guessing, but Dad probably wants to know\nmore about Yotsuba and Uesugi-kun's relationship.
Huh...
Yotsuba.\nYou haven't told Dad\nabout your relationship with Uesugi-kun, have you?
N-no, I haven't.
From our conversations and behavior,\nhe probably already had a vague idea that\nsomething special is going on between you and Yotsuba.
...Is that so?
He probably wouldn't have realized it half a year ago.
But now, he comes home to our apartment\nand eats meals with us.
(Oh, right.\n That also happened when we decided\n on our graduation trip.)
(And since the time when Nino and Itsuki\n ran away from home,\n it seems like he's become much more attentive.)
At times like these, if someone suddenly starts\ntalking about a trip to Okinawa,\nit's only natural for a parent to worry.
That's true...
(From my perspective, I was planning to deepen my\n relationship with Yotsuba a bit more\n before making a formal confession...)
But why come all the way to Okinawa?
Because that's how worried he was about Yotsuba.\nWell, a lot happened.
Ahaha...
Even so, Daddy is the type to do things like this, huh.\nIt's surprising.
Is that so?\nHe listens to us during meals and such,\neven though it seems like he's not.
(Though they're saying this and that,\n but it sounds like they're getting along well\n with their dad.)
But isn't this a good chance, Fu-kun?\nYou'll reassure us, won't you?
Huh? A chance? What do you mean, Nino?
Our father is also worried,\nso you have to reassure him.
...I see. If I don't make a decision here,\nmy manliness is on the line.
Um, Uesugi-san, what's going on with you, even?
Listen carefully, Yotsuba!
O-okay!
From now on, we will report to your dad\nand officially acknowledge our relationship!
WHAA%p-2;――%p;?!\nWHAAAAAAAAAAAT?!
I was waiting for those words, Futaro-kun.
Big bro, you're so cool!\nIt's like a romance drama!
But, but, do we have to do it here and now?!
This is also a good opportunity, isn't it?\nIt will eventually become necessary, so the only\ndifference is whether we do it sooner or later.
H-hey!\nWhy is everyone so excited about this?!
Th-then I'll tell him myself!\nUesugi-san, please wait here!
Yotsuba, wait.
Nino, don't stop me!
No. I won't let you go.
Even you, Miku?!
I won't let you do something to trouble Futaro.
Fu-kun has come this far for us, right?\nAren't you happy?
Th-that's...
Yotsuba, leave everything to me.
Uesugi-san...
Yotsuba.
Ichika...
I understand.\nI'll leave it to you.
He'll probably be opposed to us though.
Ahaha... That's right.
What the...? Don't get weak-kneed now.
Where did your momentum go from earlier?\nAre things really okay?
But isn't it reckless to just charge in\nwithout a plan and face him head on?\nThis is our dad we're talking about here.
We need to set the stage and gain the advantage.
Okay, let's go back to the hotel\nand plan our strategy.
I've got this.\nI'm not so spineless as to back down now.
I've never thought of you that way, Uesugi-san.
I know you're someone\nwho always follows through with what you say.
But isn't it reckless to just charge in\nwithout a plan and face him head on?\nThis is our dad we're talking about here.
We need to set the stage and gain the advantage.
Okay, let's go back to the hotel\nand plan our strategy.
I just need to state the facts.
Your father's reaction\nwon't have any impact on our relationship, will it?
Wha...
You think differently?
No, it's like you said, Uesugi-san.
You're pretty straightforward.\nWhat you just said really had oomph.
D-don't make fun of me....
But isn't it reckless to just charge in\nwithout a plan and face him head on?\nThis is our dad we're talking about here.
We need to set the stage and gain the advantage.
Okay, let's go back to the hotel\nand plan our strategy.
